geographicSummary: District Five spans the East and Northeast sides of Columbus, from the Linden and Near East neighborhoods up toward the Easton area and the Westerville and Gahanna borders. The district has significant racial diversity and includes both established residential communities and major commercial corridors.

pointsOfInterest.name: Easton Town Center
pointsOfInterest.description: Major open-air shopping, dining, and entertainment destination with national retailers and a movie theater.

pointsOfInterest.name: Linden Community Center
pointsOfInterest.description: State-of-the-art recreation facility with gymnasium, fitness center, water park, and swimming pool.

pointsOfInterest.name: Linden Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 40-plus acre park with splash pad, playgrounds, sports fields, fishing pond, and picnic areas.

pointsOfInterest.name: Franklin Park Conservatory and Botanical Gardens
pointsOfInterest.description: Botanical garden and conservatory with exotic plant collections, seasonal exhibitions, and outdoor gardens.

pointsOfInterest.name: Milo-Grogan Recreation Center
pointsOfInterest.description: Community recreation center offering programs, fitness facilities, and youth activities.

pointsOfInterest.name: Wolfe Park
pointsOfInterest.description: Neighborhood park in the Near East area with sports facilities and community gathering spaces.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (backfill_2021.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (build_district_profiles.py)
